message.content:
{
  "homework": {
    "sessionInformation": {
      "sessionNumber": "1",
      "sessionTitle": "Units and Measurements - Session 1",
      "subject": "Physics (Code No. 042)",
      "grade": "Class XI",
      "difficultyLevel": "Balanced",
      "learningPace": "Balanced",
      "estimatedHomeworkDuration": "45 minutes"
    },
    "homework": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "type": "Conceptual Understanding & Classification",
        "title": "Fundamental vs Derived Quantities and SI Units",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Identify fundamental quantities", "Distinguish between derived units"],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Physical Quantities",
          "SI System of Units"
        ],
        "difficulty": "Foundational",
        "marks": 5,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Review the list below. Identify which quantities are fundamental and derive their SI units based on standard definitions taught in class.",
        "question": "Classify the following physical quantities as either 'Fundamental' or 'Derived'. For each, write its corresponding SI unit symbol.\n\nList: Length, Mass, Time, Force, Area, Velocity, Energy",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Create a table with three columns: Quantity Name | Type (Fundamental/Derived) | SI Unit Symbol.",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "Length-Fundamental-m; Mass-Fundamental-kg; Time-Fundamental-s; Force-Derived-N; Area-Derived-m²; Velocity-Derived-m/s; Energy-Derived-J"
      },
      {
        "id": 2,
        "type": "Numerical Application",
        "title": "Unit Conversion and Dimensional Analysis Basics",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Convert between units of the same dimension"],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "System of Units",
          "Dimensional Consistency"
        ],
        "difficulty": "Balanced",
        "marks": 8,
        "estimatedTime": "15 minutes",
        "instructions": "Perform the following conversions. Show your working steps clearly using dimensional analysis (factor-label method). Ensure significant figures are maintained where applicable.",
        "question": "Convert the following measurements into their SI base units:\n\na) 72 km/h to m/s\nb) 50 cm² to m²\nc) 3.6 × 10⁶ seconds to days (approximate)\nd) A force of 5 Newtons expressed in terms of kg, m, and s.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Show the conversion factor used for each calculation and write the final answer with correct units.",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "a) 20 m/s; b) 0.005 m² or 5×10⁻³ m²; c) ~41.67 days (or exact calculation); d) kg·m/s²"
      },
      {
        "id": 3,
        "type": "Real-World Application & Observation",
        "title": "Measurement in Daily Life: Precision and Accuracy",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Understand the importance of standard units"],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Importance of Measurement"
        ],
        "difficulty": "Balanced",
        "marks": 6,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Think about a measurement task you performed at home or school recently (e.g., measuring height, cooking ingredients, timing an activity). Write a short reflection.",
        "question": "Describe one instance where precise measurement was important. What instrument would be best suited for this? Explain why using the concept of 'standard units' in your answer.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write 3-4 sentences describing the scenario, the tool used (or needed), and the reason standardization matters here.",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "Student describes a specific activity (e.g., measuring fabric for curtains) and explains that using non-standard units like 'hand spans' leads to errors, whereas SI units ensure consistency."
      },
      {
        "id": 4,
        "type": "Higher Order Thinking / Analysis",
        "title": "Dimensional Formula Derivation Challenge",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Derive dimensional formulas for derived quantities"],
        "topicCoverage": [
          "Dimensions of Physical Quantities"
        ],
        "difficulty": "Advanced (Challenge)",
        "marks": 6,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Using the fundamental dimensions M (Mass), L (Length), and T (Time), derive the dimensional formula for Power. Show your logical steps starting from the definition of Work.",
        "question": "Derive the dimensional formula for 'Power'.\n\nHint: Recall that Power = Work / Time, and Work = Force × Distance.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write out the derivation step-by-step ending with [M¹ L² T⁻³].",
        "visualRequirement": "",
        "expectedResponse": "Force = MLT⁻²; Work = (MLT⁻²) × L = ML²T⁻²; Power = (ML²T⁻²)/T = ML²T⁻³"
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "totalQuestions": 4,
      "totalMarks": 25,
      "estimatedCompletionTime": "45 minutes",
      "learningOutcomesCovered": [
        "Identify fundamental quantities and their SI units",
        "Distinguish between derived units",
        "Convert between different systems of units (SI)",
        "Understand the importance of standardization in measurement",
        "Derive dimensional formulas for basic physical quantities"
      ],
      "topicsCovered": [
        "Physical Quantities and Units",
        "The SI System",
        "Dimensional Analysis Basics"
      ],
      "subtopicsCovered": [
        "Fundamental vs Derived Quantities",
        "Unit Conversion Techniques",
        "Real-world application of measurement standards",
        "Derivation of Dimensional Formulas (Power)"
      ],
      "taskDistribution": {
        "Conceptual Understanding & Classification": 1,
        "Numerical Application": 1,
        "Real-World Application & Observation": 1,
        "Higher Order Thinking / Analysis": 1
      },
      "homeExperimentIncluded": false,
      "parentEngagementIncluded": true
    }
  }
}
